# smugglex

> HTTP Request Smuggling Scanner

## Pages

- [smugglex](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/): Fast, Rust-powered HTTP Request Smuggling scanner. Detect CL.TE, TE.CL, TE.TE, H2C, H2 downgrade and CL-Edge desync with timing-based detection.

## Advanced

- [Exploitation](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/advanced/exploitation/): Post-detection exploitation capabilities
- [Fingerprinting](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/advanced/fingerprinting/): Proxy and server fingerprinting
- [Fuzzing](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/advanced/fuzzing/): Mutation-based payload fuzzing
- [Pipeline](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/advanced/pipeline/): Integrating smugglex with other tools

## Checks

- [CL-Edge](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/checks/cl-edge/): Content-Length edge case attacks
- [CL.TE](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/checks/cl-te/): Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding desynchronization
- [H2](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/checks/h2/): HTTP/2 protocol smuggling
- [H2C](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/checks/h2c/): HTTP/2 Cleartext smuggling
- [TE.CL](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/checks/te-cl/): Transfer-Encoding vs Content-Length desynchronization
- [TE.TE](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/checks/te-te/): Transfer-Encoding obfuscation attacks

## Installation

- [Options](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/usage/options/): All CLI options for smugglex
- [Output](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/usage/output/): Understanding smugglex output formats
- [Quick Start](https://smugglex.hahwul.com/usage/quick-start/): Run your first smugglex scan
